roadmap.milestone_count_rationale: 5 milestones: Akshay is a beginner with existing foundational skills (full-stack, DSA) and 35 hours/week. A 5-milestone path provides balanced progression, allowing for deep dives into SDE interview readiness, backend, microservices, performance, and advanced system design, which is appropriate for a high ICP type aiming for product-based companies.

vision_profile.current_state: 3rd-year engineering student with full-stack and DSA skills.
vision_profile.main_blocker: Refining problem-solving and system design skills to meet the specific requirements of off-campus product company interviews.
vision_profile.top_motivation: Securing an off-campus placement in a product-based company.

milestones.label: M01
milestones.o: After this milestone, you will be able to confidently approach and solve medium-difficulty DSA problems and explain core system design principles relevant to product-based company interviews.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules are chosen to cover the essential pillars for initial SDE interview readiness: advanced data structures & algorithms, object-oriented design for coding problems, and foundational system design concepts.
milestones.identity_statement: You are becoming a structured problem-solver, ready to tackle technical challenges with a strong foundation in both code and design.

project.title: Design & Implement a URL Shortener Service Core
project.description: Design the core components of a URL shortener service, focusing on data structures for mapping long to short URLs, collision handling, and basic API design. Implement the core logic in your preferred language.
project.deliverable: A working command-line application or basic API endpoint for URL shortening and redirection, along with a design document outlining data structures and API choices.
seeded_errors: The hash function for short URLs produces frequent collisions for common inputs, requiring the learner to implement a robust collision resolution strategy.

modules.title: Advanced DSA for Interviews
modules.skill_count_rationale: 6 skills are needed to cover critical advanced DSA topics frequently encountered in product-based company interviews, building on existing knowledge.

skills.title: Graph Algorithms for Problem Solving
skills.why_this_skill: Graphs are fundamental in modeling real-world problems like social networks, routing, and dependencies, making them crucial for complex coding challenges.

scenario.title: Debugging a Social Network Friend Recommendation Algorithm

skills.title: Dynamic Programming Techniques
skills.why_this_skill: DP is essential for solving complex optimization problems efficiently, a common requirement in competitive programming and technical interviews.
lessons: Common DP Patterns (Knapsack, Longest Common Subsequence)

scenario.title: Optimizing a Resource Allocation Algorithm with DP

skills.title: Tree and Heap Data Structures
skills.why_this_skill: Trees and heaps are fundamental for hierarchical data, priority queues, and efficient search/sort operations, critical for many algorithms.

scenario.title: Implementing a Custom Priority Queue for Task Scheduling

skills.title: Hashing and String Algorithms
skills.why_this_skill: Hashing is crucial for efficient data retrieval and storage, while string algorithms are vital for text processing and pattern matching in various applications.
lessons: Prefix Sums and Sliding Window for Strings

scenario.title: Detecting Plagiarism in Large Text Corpora

skills.title: Greedy Algorithms
skills.why_this_skill: Greedy algorithms offer simple, efficient solutions for certain optimization problems, often appearing in interviews to test problem-solving intuition.

scenario.title: Optimizing Change-Making for a Vending Machine

skills.title: Backtracking and Recursion
skills.why_this_skill: Recursion and backtracking are powerful techniques for exploring solution spaces, essential for problems like permutations, combinations, and Sudoku solvers.

scenario.title: Solving N-Queens Problem with Backtracking

science.desc: A critical bug in a graph-based feature recommendation system is causing infinite loops for certain user profiles. You need to debug and fix the graph traversal logic.
A new feature requires calculating the optimal path for delivery drivers, but the current greedy approach fails in complex road networks. You must propose and implement a more robust algorithm.

modules.title: Object-Oriented Design & Problem Solving
modules.skill_count_rationale: 6 skills are necessary to instill strong object-oriented design principles and patterns, which are crucial for writing maintainable, scalable code in product-based companies.

skills.title: Core OOP Principles
skills.why_this_skill: Mastering OOP principles is foundational for designing modular, reusable, and maintainable software systems, a key expectation in professional development.

scenario.title: Refactoring a Monolithic Payment Gateway into Modular Components

skills.title: SOLID Principles for Clean Code
skills.why_this_skill: SOLID principles guide the design of robust, maintainable, and scalable software, preventing common pitfalls in large codebases.

scenario.title: Redesigning a Notification Service to Adhere to OCP

skills.title: Creational Design Patterns
skills.why_this_skill: Creational patterns provide flexible ways to create objects, decoupling client code from the concrete classes it instantiates, improving system flexibility.
lessons: Builder Pattern for Complex Object Construction

scenario.title: Designing a Flexible Document Converter with Factory Pattern

skills.title: Structural Design Patterns
skills.why_this_skill: Structural patterns simplify the design by identifying a simple way to realize relationships between entities, making systems more flexible and efficient.

scenario.title: Integrating a Legacy Logging System with a Modern Application using Adapter

skills.title: Code Refactoring and Clean Code Practices
skills.why_this_skill: Writing clean, readable, and maintainable code is a hallmark of a professional developer, reducing technical debt and improving collaboration.
lessons: Code Smells and How to Address Them

scenario.title: Refactoring a Heavily Coupled Legacy Module for Better Maintainability

skills.title: Unit Testing and Debugging Strategies
skills.why_this_skill: Effective testing and debugging are critical for delivering high-quality, reliable software and efficiently resolving issues in production.

scenario.title: Debugging a Production Bug Caused by an Edge Case Not Covered by Tests

science.desc: You are tasked with designing a new 'User Management' module. The existing system has tightly coupled components. You need to propose an OOP design that adheres to SOLID principles for future scalability.

modules.title: Foundations of System Design
modules.skill_count_rationale: 6 skills are selected to provide a comprehensive introduction to fundamental system design concepts, enabling the learner to understand and discuss basic architectural tradeoffs.

skills.title: Introduction to System Design
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding system design is crucial for building scalable and robust applications, and a common component of SDE interviews at product companies.
lessons: Key Components of a Distributed System

skills.title: Scalability Concepts
skills.why_this_skill: Designing for scalability is paramount in modern applications to handle increasing user loads and data volumes without performance degradation.

skills.title: Availability and Reliability
skills.why_this_skill: Ensuring a system is always accessible and performs its functions correctly is critical for user trust and business continuity.

skills.title: Latency and Throughput Optimization
skills.why_this_skill: Optimizing for low latency and high throughput directly impacts user experience and system efficiency, crucial for competitive product performance.

skills.title: Data Storage Choices
skills.why_this_skill: Selecting the right database is critical for system performance, scalability, and data integrity, requiring an understanding of various storage paradigms.
lessons: Choosing the Right Database for Your Use Case

skills.title: API Design Principles
skills.why_this_skill: Well-designed APIs are the backbone of modern distributed systems, ensuring clear communication and ease of integration between services.
lessons: Idempotency and Error Handling in APIs

science.desc: Design a system to store and retrieve user profiles for a social media application, considering scalability and availability for 1 million daily active users.

milestones.label: M02
milestones.o: After this milestone, you will be able to design, implement, and optimize RESTful APIs, manage databases, and understand basic distributed system challenges for scalable backend services.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules are chosen to provide a focused deep dive into backend development, covering API design, database management, and the fundamental challenges of distributed systems.
milestones.identity_statement: You are building the robust infrastructure that powers applications, becoming adept at creating efficient and reliable backend services.

project.title: Build a Scalable E-commerce Product Catalog API
project.description: Develop a backend API for an e-commerce product catalog. Design the database schema, implement RESTful endpoints for product listing, search, and detail retrieval, and ensure basic scalability considerations.
project.deliverable: A deployed backend API (e.g., using Flask/Django or Node.js/Express) with a connected database, accessible via HTTP requests, and a README explaining the API endpoints and database schema.
seeded_errors: The initial database schema design leads to N+1 query problems for product listings with associated categories and reviews, requiring the learner to optimize queries or redesign relationships.

modules.title: API Design & Development
modules.skill_count_rationale: 6 skills are required to cover the full lifecycle of API development, from design principles to implementation and security, ensuring a comprehensive understanding.

skills.title: Building RESTful APIs
skills.why_this_skill: RESTful APIs are the standard for web service communication, essential for building interoperable and scalable backend systems.
lessons: Principles of REST and HTTP Methods
Implementing REST APIs with a Framework (e.g., Flask/Express)

scenario.title: Designing a Versioned API for a Growing Mobile Application

skills.title: API Authentication and Authorization
skills.why_this_skill: Securing APIs is critical to protect sensitive data and ensure only authorized users can access resources, a non-negotiable in product development.

scenario.title: Implementing JWT Authentication for a Microservice API Gateway

skills.title: API Rate Limiting and Throttling
skills.why_this_skill: Controlling API access rates prevents abuse, ensures fair usage, and protects backend services from overload, maintaining system stability.

scenario.title: Preventing API Abuse from a Malicious Botnet Attack

skills.title: API Documentation and Testing
skills.why_this_skill: Clear documentation and robust testing are essential for API usability, maintainability, and ensuring correct functionality for consumers.

skills.title: API Gateway Patterns
skills.why_this_skill: API Gateways centralize common API concerns like routing, authentication, and rate limiting, simplifying microservice architectures and improving manageability.

skills.title: GraphQL Basics
skills.why_this_skill: GraphQL offers a flexible alternative to REST, allowing clients to request exactly the data they need, reducing over-fetching and under-fetching.
lessons: Introduction to GraphQL Queries and Mutations

science.desc: A critical API endpoint is experiencing high latency and frequent timeouts under peak load. You need to identify the bottleneck and propose solutions, potentially involving rate limiting or caching.

modules.title: Database Design & Optimization
modules.skill_count_rationale: 6 skills are essential to cover comprehensive database knowledge, from schema design and query optimization to understanding different database types and their use cases.

skills.title: Relational Database Design
skills.why_this_skill: Relational databases are ubiquitous, and proper schema design is fundamental for data integrity, efficient querying, and application performance.

scenario.title: Redesigning a Product Review Schema to Support Multi-Language Reviews

skills.title: SQL Query Optimization
skills.why_this_skill: Efficient SQL queries are vital for database performance, directly impacting application responsiveness and scalability.

scenario.title: Optimizing a Slow Reporting Query on a Large E-commerce Database

skills.title: NoSQL Databases
skills.why_this_skill: NoSQL databases offer flexibility and scalability for specific use cases, complementing relational databases in modern architectures.

skills.title: ORM and Database Interaction
skills.why_this_skill: ORMs simplify database interactions in application code, improving developer productivity and reducing boilerplate SQL.

skills.title: Database Security Best Practices
skills.why_this_skill: Protecting sensitive data stored in databases is paramount, requiring knowledge of common vulnerabilities and mitigation strategies.
lessons: Data Encryption at Rest and In Transit

skills.title: Distributed Transactions and Consistency
skills.why_this_skill: Managing data consistency across multiple databases in distributed systems is a complex but critical challenge for data integrity.

science.desc: A new feature requires storing highly unstructured user activity logs. The current relational database is struggling. You need to recommend and justify a NoSQL solution.
A critical database operation involving multiple services is failing intermittently due to consistency issues. You need to identify the cause and propose a distributed transaction pattern.

modules.title: Distributed Systems Fundamentals
modules.skill_count_rationale: 6 skills are chosen to provide a foundational understanding of distributed systems, covering common challenges, communication patterns, and essential design considerations.

skills.title: Challenges in Distributed Systems
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding the inherent complexities of distributed systems (e.g., network latency, partial failures) is crucial for designing resilient architectures.

skills.title: Inter-Service Communication
skills.why_this_skill: Choosing appropriate communication mechanisms between services is vital for performance, reliability, and maintainability in distributed architectures.

skills.title: Data Replication and Consistency Models
skills.why_this_skill: Managing data consistency across replicated nodes is a core challenge in distributed systems, impacting both availability and correctness.

skills.title: Fault Tolerance and Resilience
skills.why_this_skill: Designing systems that can gracefully handle failures is crucial for maintaining high availability and reliability in production environments.

skills.title: Distributed Caching
skills.why_this_skill: Caching is a powerful technique to improve performance and reduce database load in distributed systems by storing frequently accessed data closer to the application.

skills.title: Leader Election and Consensus
skills.why_this_skill: Consensus algorithms are fundamental for coordinating actions and maintaining consistency among nodes in a distributed system, ensuring reliability.

science.desc: Design a distributed key-value store like Amazon DynamoDB, focusing on data consistency, availability, and fault tolerance.

milestones.label: M03
milestones.o: After this milestone, you will be able to architect and deploy microservices-based applications on cloud platforms, ensuring basic security and reliability.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules are selected to guide the learner through the transition to cloud-native development, focusing on microservices architecture, practical cloud deployment, and essential networking/security for distributed applications.
milestones.identity_statement: You are evolving into a cloud architect, capable of building and deploying scalable, resilient applications in modern cloud environments.

project.title: Deploy a Microservices-Based Chat Application on Cloud
project.description: Architect and deploy a simple real-time chat application using a microservices approach. Implement separate services for user management, message handling, and a gateway. Containerize services and deploy them on a cloud platform (e.g., AWS EC2/ECS, GCP Compute Engine/Cloud Run).
project.deliverable: A deployed, functional chat application with multiple microservices, accessible via a public URL, along with a deployment script (e.g., Docker Compose, basic Terraform) and architecture diagram.
seeded_errors: Inter-service communication between microservices fails intermittently due to incorrect network configurations or service discovery issues, requiring debugging of cloud networking rules.

modules.title: Microservices Architecture
modules.skill_count_rationale: 6 skills are chosen to provide a comprehensive understanding of microservices, covering their principles, communication patterns, and common design challenges.

skills.title: Microservices Principles and Patterns
skills.why_this_skill: Microservices enable independent development, deployment, and scaling of services, crucial for agility and resilience in large-scale applications.

scenario.title: Decomposing a Legacy Monolithic E-commerce Application into Microservices

skills.title: Service Discovery and Registration
skills.why_this_skill: In microservices, services need to find each other dynamically, making service discovery a critical component for flexible and scalable architectures.

scenario.title: Troubleshooting a Microservice That Cannot Find Its Dependencies

skills.title: Event-Driven Architecture
skills.why_this_skill: Event-driven architectures promote loose coupling and scalability, enabling services to react to events without direct dependencies, ideal for complex microservice ecosystems.

scenario.title: Designing an Order Processing System Using Event Sourcing

skills.title: Distributed Tracing and Logging
skills.why_this_skill: In microservices, tracing requests across multiple services is essential for debugging, performance monitoring, and understanding system behavior.

skills.title: Containerization with Docker
skills.why_this_skill: Docker is the industry standard for packaging and deploying applications, providing consistency across environments and simplifying microservice deployments.

skills.title: Kubernetes Basics
skills.why_this_skill: Kubernetes is the leading container orchestration platform, essential for managing and scaling microservices in production environments.

science.desc: A microservice deployment is failing because the Docker image cannot be pulled from the registry. You need to debug the Dockerfile and registry access permissions.
A new feature requires real-time updates across multiple services. You need to design an event-driven communication pattern using a message broker.

modules.title: Cloud & Deployment Strategies
modules.skill_count_rationale: 6 skills are necessary to provide practical knowledge of cloud deployment, covering CI/CD, infrastructure as code, and serverless computing, essential for modern SDE roles.

skills.title: Continuous Integration & Deployment (CI/CD)
skills.why_this_skill: CI/CD pipelines automate the software delivery process, enabling faster, more reliable releases and reducing manual errors.
lessons: Building a Basic CI Pipeline (e.g., Jenkins, GitHub Actions)

skills.title: Infrastructure as Code (IaC)
skills.why_this_skill: IaC allows managing and provisioning infrastructure through code, ensuring consistency, repeatability, and version control for cloud resources.
lessons: Managing State and Modules in IaC

skills.title: Serverless Computing (FaaS)
skills.why_this_skill: Serverless architectures reduce operational overhead and scale automatically, making them ideal for event-driven microservices and cost-efficient deployments.
lessons: Introduction to Serverless (AWS Lambda, Google Cloud Functions)

skills.title: Cloud Storage Solutions
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding various cloud storage options is crucial for selecting the right solution for different data types, access patterns, and cost requirements.

skills.title: Cloud Networking Basics
skills.why_this_skill: A solid understanding of cloud networking is essential for securely connecting services, managing traffic, and ensuring proper communication within cloud environments.
lessons: Subnets, Route Tables, and Security Groups
Load Balancers and DNS in Cloud

skills.title: Cloud Cost Optimization
skills.why_this_skill: Managing cloud costs effectively is a critical skill for SDEs, ensuring efficient resource utilization and preventing unnecessary expenditures.
lessons: Monitoring and Alerting for Cost Anomalies

science.desc: A new microservice needs to be deployed with a CI/CD pipeline. You need to configure the pipeline to build, test, and deploy the service to a Kubernetes cluster.

modules.title: Networking & Security Basics
modules.skill_count_rationale: 6 skills are chosen to provide foundational knowledge in networking and security, crucial for building secure and well-connected applications in any environment.

skills.title: TCP/IP and HTTP Fundamentals
skills.why_this_skill: A deep understanding of TCP/IP and HTTP is foundational for anyone building networked applications, enabling effective debugging and optimization.

skills.title: DNS and Load Balancing
skills.why_this_skill: DNS and load balancing are critical for routing traffic efficiently and reliably to distributed services, ensuring high availability and performance.

video.title: Traffic Management with DNS and Load Balancers

skills.title: Network Security and Firewalls
skills.why_this_skill: Protecting network boundaries and controlling traffic flow is fundamental to securing applications from external threats and unauthorized access.

skills.title: SSL/TLS and Encryption
skills.why_this_skill: SSL/TLS encrypts communication over networks, protecting data privacy and integrity, a fundamental requirement for secure web applications.

skills.title: Web Application Security (OWASP Top 10)
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding common web vulnerabilities and how to prevent them is crucial for building secure applications and protecting user data.

skills.title: Identity and Access Management (IAM)
skills.why_this_skill: Proper IAM ensures that only authenticated and authorized entities (users, services) can access resources, critical for cloud security.

science.desc: Explain how you would secure a web application deployed in the cloud from common vulnerabilities like SQL injection and XSS, and discuss your approach to IAM.

milestones.label: M04
milestones.o: After this milestone, you will be able to identify and resolve performance bottlenecks, implement caching strategies, and set up monitoring for production systems.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules are chosen to focus on advanced aspects of system reliability and performance, covering optimization techniques, robust observability, and complex concurrency challenges.
milestones.identity_statement: You are becoming a guardian of system health, ensuring applications are not only functional but also fast, reliable, and observable under any condition.

project.title: Optimize and Monitor a High-Traffic API Service
project.description: Take an existing API service (e.g., the product catalog from M02) and implement performance optimizations like caching, database query tuning, and connection pooling. Integrate monitoring and alerting to track key metrics (latency, error rates, resource utilization).
project.deliverable: An optimized API service with demonstrable performance improvements (e.g., benchmark results), integrated with a monitoring dashboard (e.g., Prometheus/Grafana, CloudWatch) and configured alerts.
seeded_errors: The caching strategy implemented leads to stale data being served to users under certain conditions, requiring the learner to refine cache invalidation logic.

modules.title: Scalability & Performance Optimization
modules.skill_count_rationale: 6 skills are selected to provide a deep understanding of advanced scalability and performance techniques, crucial for optimizing high-traffic production systems.

skills.title: Advanced Load Balancing
skills.why_this_skill: Sophisticated load balancing ensures optimal resource utilization, high availability, and efficient traffic distribution in large-scale distributed systems.

video.title: Optimizing Traffic with Advanced Load Balancers

scenario.title: Configuring a Load Balancer for Blue/Green Deployments

skills.title: Advanced Caching Strategies
skills.why_this_skill: Effective caching is critical for reducing database load and improving response times, especially for read-heavy applications.

scenario.title: Resolving Stale Data Issues in a Globally Distributed Cache

skills.title: Database Sharding and Partitioning
skills.why_this_skill: Sharding and partitioning are advanced techniques for scaling databases horizontally, essential for handling massive datasets and high transaction volumes.

scenario.title: Designing a Sharding Strategy for a Multi-Tenant SaaS Application

skills.title: Performance Profiling and Tuning
skills.why_this_skill: Identifying and eliminating performance bottlenecks through profiling is a critical skill for optimizing application speed and resource usage.
lessons: Tools for Application Profiling (e.g., cProfile, VisualVM)

skills.title: Queueing Theory in System Design
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding queueing theory helps predict system behavior under load, optimize resource allocation, and prevent bottlenecks in distributed systems.
lessons: Impact of Queue Length on Latency

video.title: Applying Queueing Theory to System Design

skills.title: Content Delivery Networks (CDNs)
skills.why_this_skill: CDNs significantly improve content delivery speed and reduce server load by caching assets geographically closer to users, enhancing user experience.
lessons: CDN Cache Invalidation and Edge Logic

science.desc: A critical API endpoint is experiencing intermittent spikes in latency, but only during specific times of the day. You need to use profiling tools to identify the root cause.
A new marketing campaign is expected to generate 10x traffic. You need to propose a database scaling strategy using sharding to handle the load.

modules.title: Reliability & Observability
modules.skill_count_rationale: 6 skills are crucial for building and maintaining reliable, observable systems, covering monitoring, alerting, and incident response, essential for production SDEs.

skills.title: Monitoring and Alerting Systems
skills.why_this_skill: Robust monitoring and alerting are essential for detecting issues early, understanding system health, and ensuring rapid incident response.
lessons: Key Metrics for System Health (RED, USE)

scenario.title: Configuring Alerts for a Sudden Spike in API Error Rates

skills.title: Centralized Logging
skills.why_this_skill: Centralized logging aggregates logs from all services, making it easier to debug issues, perform root cause analysis, and monitor system behavior.

scenario.title: Diagnosing a Production Issue by Analyzing Distributed Logs Across Microservices

skills.title: Advanced Distributed Tracing
skills.why_this_skill: Distributed tracing provides end-to-end visibility into requests flowing through complex microservice architectures, invaluable for performance debugging.

skills.title: Chaos Engineering
skills.why_this_skill: Chaos engineering proactively identifies weaknesses in systems by injecting controlled failures, improving resilience before incidents occur.

video.title: Building Resilient Systems with Chaos Engineering

skills.title: Incident Management and Postmortems
skills.why_this_skill: Effective incident response and post-incident analysis are crucial for minimizing downtime, learning from failures, and continuously improving system reliability.

video.title: Managing Incidents and Learning from Failures

skills.title: Service Level Objectives (SLOs)
skills.why_this_skill: Defining and monitoring SLOs provides a clear, data-driven approach to managing service reliability and aligning engineering efforts with business goals.

science.desc: A critical production service is down. You are on-call and need to follow an incident response playbook to diagnose the issue using logs and metrics, and restore service.
The error budget for a user authentication service is being depleted rapidly. You need to identify the root causes of the errors and propose solutions to improve reliability.

modules.title: Advanced Concurrency & Multithreading
modules.skill_count_rationale: 6 skills are chosen to provide a deep understanding of concurrency, covering advanced concepts, synchronization primitives, and common pitfalls, essential for high-performance systems.

skills.title: Concurrency vs. Parallelism
skills.why_this_skill: Distinguishing between concurrency and parallelism is fundamental for designing efficient and responsive applications that leverage multi-core processors.
lessons: Use Cases for Concurrency and Parallelism

skills.title: Thread Synchronization Primitives
skills.why_this_skill: Proper synchronization is essential to prevent race conditions and ensure data consistency when multiple threads access shared resources.

skills.title: Deadlock, Livelock, and Starvation
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding and preventing these concurrency issues is vital for building reliable multi-threaded applications that don't freeze or become unresponsive.

skills.title: Memory Models and Visibility
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding how memory is shared and synchronized across threads is crucial for writing correct and performant concurrent code, especially in low-level programming.
lessons: Java Memory Model (JMM) / C++ Memory Model

skills.title: Thread Pools and Executors
skills.why_this_skill: Thread pools efficiently manage and reuse threads, reducing overhead and improving performance for applications with many concurrent tasks.

video.title: Efficient Task Management with Thread Pools

skills.title: Lock-Free and Wait-Free Algorithms
skills.why_this_skill: Lock-free algorithms offer maximum concurrency and performance by avoiding traditional locks, crucial for ultra-low latency and high-throughput systems.

science.desc: Design a highly concurrent, thread-safe data structure (e.g., a concurrent hash map) and explain how you would handle synchronization and avoid common concurrency issues.

milestones.label: M05
milestones.o: After this milestone, you will be able to confidently articulate complex system designs, solve advanced coding problems under pressure, and lead technical discussions in interviews, ready for a product-based company role.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules are chosen to consolidate all learned skills, focusing on advanced system design case studies, intensive problem-solving for interviews, and critical behavioral/communication skills to ensure readiness for product-based SDE roles.
milestones.identity_statement: You are now a seasoned problem-solver and system architect, ready to excel in the most challenging technical interviews and contribute immediately to a product team.

project.title: Design and Implement a Real-time Recommendation Engine
project.description: Design a real-time recommendation engine (e.g., for products, news articles, or videos). Focus on data ingestion, processing, storage, and serving recommendations. Consider scalability, latency, and data freshness. Implement a simplified version of the core logic.
project.deliverable: A comprehensive system design document, a working prototype of the recommendation service (e.g., using a stream processing framework like Kafka Streams or Flink, and a NoSQL database), and a presentation of the design and implementation choices.
seeded_errors: The real-time recommendation updates are not propagating correctly, leading to stale recommendations for users. The learner must debug the data pipeline and consistency model.

modules.title: System Design Case Studies
modules.skill_count_rationale: 6 skills are essential to cover a range of complex system design scenarios, allowing the learner to apply all previously acquired knowledge to real-world problems and articulate sophisticated solutions.

skills.title: Designing YouTube/Netflix
skills.why_this_skill: Designing large-scale video streaming platforms covers complex challenges in content delivery, storage, encoding, and recommendation systems.

scenario.title: Optimizing Video Playback Latency for Global Users

skills.title: Designing Twitter/Facebook Feed
skills.why_this_skill: Designing social media feeds involves challenges in fan-out, real-time updates, and handling massive read/write loads.
lessons: Fan-out on Write vs. Fan-out on Read

scenario.title: Scaling a Social Media News Feed to Support Billions of Users

skills.title: Designing a Distributed Chat System
skills.why_this_skill: Chat systems involve real-time communication, message delivery guarantees, and presence management, offering insights into complex distributed interactions.

scenario.title: Ensuring Message Delivery Guarantees in a High-Volume Chat System

skills.title: Designing a Payment Gateway
skills.why_this_skill: Payment gateways involve critical aspects of security, atomicity, and fault tolerance, making them excellent case studies for robust system design.

skills.title: Designing a Search Engine
skills.why_this_skill: Search engines involve complex challenges in indexing, ranking, and distributed data retrieval, offering a holistic view of large-scale system design.

skills.title: Designing a Distributed File System
skills.why_this_skill: Distributed file systems present challenges in data consistency, fault tolerance, and large-scale storage, providing insights into fundamental infrastructure design.
lessons: Data Replication and Consistency in DFS

science.desc: A new feature requires real-time search capabilities for user-generated content. You need to design a scalable search index and query service.
A critical data loss event occurred in a distributed storage system. You need to propose a robust data replication and recovery strategy.

modules.title: Advanced Interview Problem Solving
modules.skill_count_rationale: 6 skills are chosen to provide intensive practice on the most challenging and frequently asked DSA topics in top product company interviews, ensuring mastery under pressure.

skills.title: Advanced Dynamic Programming Problems
skills.why_this_skill: Mastering complex DP problems is a hallmark of strong algorithmic skills, often differentiating candidates in competitive interviews.

skills.title: Hard Graph Algorithms
skills.why_this_skill: Advanced graph problems test a candidate's ability to model complex relationships and apply sophisticated algorithms, crucial for challenging interviews.
lessons: Flow Networks and Max Flow Min Cut

skills.title: Segment Trees and Fenwick Trees
skills.why_this_skill: These advanced data structures are essential for efficiently handling range queries and updates, common in competitive programming and specialized applications.
lessons: Applications in Range Queries and Updates

video.title: Advanced Data Structures for Range Queries

skills.title: Advanced String Algorithms and Suffix Structures
skills.why_this_skill: Sophisticated string algorithms are vital for bioinformatics, text processing, and pattern matching in large datasets, often appearing in advanced interviews.

skills.title: Geometric Algorithms
skills.why_this_skill: Geometric algorithms are used in graphics, robotics, and mapping, and test a unique blend of mathematical and algorithmic thinking.

skills.title: Interview Problem Patterns and Strategies
skills.why_this_skill: Recognizing common problem patterns and applying strategic approaches is key to efficiently solving diverse coding challenges under interview pressure.
lessons: Time and Space Complexity Analysis Mastery

science.desc: You are given a complex problem involving finding the shortest path in a weighted graph with negative cycles. You need to identify the appropriate algorithm and handle edge cases.
A coding interview problem requires optimizing a solution from O(N^2) to O(N log N) using an advanced data structure. You must identify the structure and implement the optimized solution.

modules.title: Behavioral & Communication Skills for SDE
modules.skill_count_rationale: 6 skills are chosen to cover essential non-technical aspects of SDE interviews, focusing on communication, teamwork, and career readiness, which are crucial for securing and succeeding in product roles.

skills.title: Effective Technical Communication
skills.why_this_skill: Clear and concise communication is vital for explaining complex technical concepts, collaborating with teams, and performing well in interviews.

skills.title: Behavioral Interview Preparation
skills.why_this_skill: Behavioral interviews assess soft skills, cultural fit, and past experiences, which are equally important as technical skills for landing a job.
lessons: STAR Method for Answering Behavioral Questions

skills.title: Salary Negotiation Strategies
skills.why_this_skill: Effective negotiation can significantly impact career earnings and job satisfaction, a crucial skill for any professional.

skills.title: Resume and LinkedIn Optimization
skills.why_this_skill: A compelling resume and professional online presence are essential for attracting recruiters and securing interview opportunities.

skills.title: Networking and Job Search Strategies
skills.why_this_skill: Effective networking and strategic job searching are crucial for discovering hidden opportunities and gaining referrals in a competitive job market.

skills.title: Full-Stack Mock Interview Practice
skills.why_this_skill: Extensive mock interview practice simulates real interview conditions, building confidence and refining both technical and communication skills under pressure.

science.desc: Participate in a full-length mock interview covering advanced DSA, system design, and behavioral questions, followed by detailed feedback on all aspects.
